title: In Rare Show of Weakness, Modi Bows to India’s Farmers
content: A bungled response to Covid and a struggling economy have hurt his party’s standing, leaving it vulnerable to a well-organized protest movement.

more_info.byline: By Emily Schmall, Karan Deep Singh and Sameer Yasir

en_title: In Rare Show of Weakness, Modi Bows to India’s Farmers
en_content: A bungled response to Covid and a struggling economy have hurt his party’s standing, leaving it vulnerable to a well-organized protest movement.

title: A Tabloid ‘Game of Thrones’ in London Could Tilt U.K. Politics
content: The top editor is out at The Daily Mail, widely viewed as the voice of middle-class voters, and the paper’s coverage of Boris Johnson’s government might be softened because of it.
en_title: A Tabloid ‘Game of Thrones’ in London Could Tilt U.K. Politics
en_content: The top editor is out at The Daily Mail, widely viewed as the voice of middle-class voters, and the paper’s coverage of Boris Johnson’s government might be softened because of it.

title: Beaver Dams Mean No Love Lost for Canada’s Emblematic Animal
content: Blamed for flooded fields, damaged roads and the occasional death, the beaver, which has played a seminal role in Canadian history, is now viewed by many as a problem, not a point of national pride.
en_title: Beaver Dams Mean No Love Lost for Canada’s Emblematic Animal
en_content: Blamed for flooded fields, damaged roads and the occasional death, the beaver, which has played a seminal role in Canadian history, is now viewed by many as a problem, not a point of national pride.

title: U.S. Tries to Convince Arab Allies It Isn’t Abandoning Them

title: What Prompted the Farm Protests in India?
content: The government withstood months of protest, with both sides refusing to compromise, before it unexpectedly bowed to the farmers’ demands this week.

title: After 15 Months in U.S. Prisons, She Now Sits in Russia’s Parliament
content: Maria Butina, convicted of serving as an unregistered foreign agent before and after the 2016 election, insists she “wasn’t a spy” and that her Duma seat is “not a reward.” Her critics call her a Kremlin “trophy.”
en_title: After 15 Months in U.S. Prisons, She Now Sits in Russia’s Parliament

title: Austria Announces Covid Vaccine Mandate, Crossing a Threshold for Europe
content: The extraordinary step shows that governments desperate to safeguard public health and economic recoveries are increasingly willing to push for once unthinkable measures.

title: Amazon Deforestation Soars to 15-Year High
content: Brazil committed this month to end illegal deforestation in eight years, but a government report raises questions about its intent and ability to meet that target.

title: Bloodied Venezuelan Opposition Returns to Elections for First Time in Years

title: Belarus Clears Migrant Camp, Easing Border Standoff With Poland
content: Throngs of people, mainly from the Mideast, who had been living in freezing conditions on the border, have been moved to a giant warehouse. Their fate is uncertain.

title: How Hunter Biden’s Firm Helped Secure Cobalt for the Chinese
content: The president’s son was part owner of a venture involved in the $3.8 billion purchase by a Chinese conglomerate of one of the world’s largest cobalt deposits. The metal is a key ingredient in batteries for electric vehicles.
